item.title: Paper accepted ! Theory of Whose Mind? Exposing the Shortcomings of One of HRI’s Core Concepts
item.description:
I am really proud to tell you that our paper about the Theory of Mind, its shortcomings, and its importance for Social Robotics, has been accepted at alt.HRI and is now available for reading here.

In this paper, we look at the origins of Theory of Mind and how it is used by the Social Robotics Community. We then address the issues that the concept is facing and why it should be abandonned as a unifying theory, and what this means for social robotics research! If you are interested in getting a critical lens on one of HRI’s core concept, have a look!

item.title: Visual Noun Modifiers: The Problem of Binding Visual and Linguistic Cues
Paper accepted ! From Corrective to Empowering Technologies: Integrating Critical Perspectives in Social Robotics for Autism
item.description: Our new extended abstract titled “From Corrective to Empowering Technologies: Integrating Critical Perspectives in Social Robotics for Autism” has been accepted for presentation at the Inter.HAI workshop! In this paper, we look at how the social robotics field usually look at autism as a problem that needs to be corrected, and introduce two approaches that are of interest for social robotics researchers who want to adopt a more critical approach. You can access the paper here.

item.title: From Corrective to Empowering Technologies: Integrating Critical Perspectives in Social Robotics for Autism
